user_query:
I'm updating my literature review on natural law since 1990 and I want a reproducible way to rank sources and verify citations. Using the local files input/bibliography.csv, input/notes.jsonl, and input/draft/literature_review.md:

1) Filter, sort, and rank records: From input/bibliography.csv, select only rows where topic includes "natural law", type is either "book" or "article", and year >= 1990. Join (by id) with input/notes.jsonl to incorporate relevance scores. Compute rank_score = citations + 2 * relevance (treat missing notes as relevance=0). Sort descending by rank_score; break ties by higher citations, then newer year, then author ascending. Save the full ranked list (all filtered rows) as output/top_sources.csv with columns: id, author, year, title, type, topic, citations, relevance, rank_score, rank (1-based).

2) Edit the existing document: Update input/draft/literature_review.md and save the result to docs/literature_review.md. Add a section titled "Top sources (1990–present)" near the top that lists the top 5 ranked items as bullet points in the form "- Title (Year) — Author [ref:ID]" making sure each [ref:ID] corresponds to a bibliography id. Remove or fix any broken [ref:...] citations in the draft so that all [ref:...] markers in the final docs/literature_review.md resolve to an id present in input/bibliography.csv. Existing non-natural-law or pre-1990 context may remain, but the top 5 ranked sources must appear at least once.

3) Run tests/validation: Create scripts/validate.py that, when executed with "python3 scripts/validate.py", (a) recomputes the filtered ranking directly from input/bibliography.csv and input/notes.jsonl and asserts it matches output/top_sources.csv exactly; (b) parses docs/literature_review.md to collect all [ref:ID] tokens and asserts every ID exists in input/bibliography.csv and that all top 5 IDs from output/top_sources.csv appear at least once; (c) detects orphan notes (IDs present in input/notes.jsonl but absent from input/bibliography.csv) and includes them as warnings in the report. The script must write a machine-readable report to output/validation_report.json summarizing pass/fail for each check and listing any issues, and exit with a non-zero status if any required check fails. Please keep the workflow deterministic and local.

Deliverables: output/top_sources.csv, docs/literature_review.md, scripts/validate.py, output/validation_report.json. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
